question:
The probability of having various numbers of children in a family in a certain
community is given by
$$
\begin{array}{lllll}
\text { Number of children: } & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
\text { Probability: } & 0.20 & 0.50 & 0.25 & 0.05
\end{array}
$$
Assume a 50–50 chance that a child born will be a boy or girl.
(i) Find the probability that the family has only one boy.
(ii) What is the probability that there are two children in the family given the
family has one boy?
answer:
(i) $P\{1$ boy $\}=\sum_{i=0}^3 P\{1$ boy $\mid i\} P\{i\}=0.3938$.
(ii) Let A denote the event that there are two children in the family and B the event that the family has one boy.

$$
P\{A \mid B\}=\frac{P\{A \cap B\}}{P\{B\}}=\frac{(0.5)(0.25)}{0.3938}=0.317
$$
